layer-2.Description: Layer 2 refers to several approaches to handle transactions off the main Ethereum chain. These allow for higher scalability of the network and reduced transaction fees. Examples include Optimistic and Zero-Knowledge (ZK) Rollups, Validium, Plasma and State Channels.

nitro.Description: Arbitrum Nitro is a second-generation Layer 2 blockchain protocol by Arbitrum which could be implemented on any blockchain system that support smart contract functionality.

bancor.Description: Bancor is a decentralized liquidity network that allows you to hold any token and convert it to any other token in the network, with no counterparty, at an automatically calculated price, using a simple web wallet.
